intro: South Korea's deputy PM warned that AI-driven labor conflicts will intensify as major companies emerge, calling for an "AI-inclusive society" following Samsung's strike. OpenAI is hiring a researcher to prepare for self-training AI systems, as the company aims to automate its own research processes. Marc Andreessen argued that AI coding agents outperform human employees because they avoid issues like illness and HR complaints.

whitelist.title: South Korea's deputy PM says AI wealth must benefit the public. The Samsung strike showed why.
whitelist.desc: Deputy PM Bae warned that AI-driven labour conflicts will keep happening as "super-large companies" emerge and called for an "AI-inclusive society."

whitelist.title: OpenAI will pay up to $445,000 for a researcher who can prepare for a world where AI trains itself
whitelist.desc: OpenAI and Sam Altman aim to automate AI research. They are now hiring for a role to prepare the company for self-training AI.

whitelist.title: Marc Andreessen says AI coding agents are better than employees because they never get 'drunk, sick' or file 'HR complaints'
whitelist.desc: "The bots never get frustrated with you," Marc Andreessen told Joe Rogan.

whitelist.title: Putting The Senses In AI
whitelist.desc: MIT panelists explored sensory AI, multimodal learning, privacy risks, robotics, and future human-machine interactions.

whitelist.desc: The shift from on-premises computing to software as a service changed the technology model and forced information technology organizatios to modernize how it builds, buys and operates software.It
